raw_text: ABAYOMI-COLE, A. B.—b. 1907; ed. Govt. Model Sch., Prince of Wales Sch., S.L., and Durham Univ.; M.O., S.L., 1944.

ABBOTT, A. W., C.M.G. (1956), C.B.E. (1949).—b. 1893; ed. Blundell's; entd. C.A. off. after compet. exam., 1912; mil. serv., 1917-19; lieut.; asst. hd., fin. dept., 1929; dep. hd., 1933; hd., 1938; sec., E.A. currency bd., 1930-38; estab. offr., 1948; sec., C.As., 1954; supmny. sec., 1958.

ABBOTT, B., O.B.E. (1957).—b. 1913; ed. King Edward's Sch. and Tynemouth High Sch.; mil. serv., 1939-46; lt.-col.; cadet, Nig., 1946; admin. offr., cl. I, W. Nig., 1954; sec. to comsn. on the public service, Nig., 1954-55; admin. offr., cl. I, 1957.

ABBOTT, B. A.—b. 1914; ed. King's Sch., King's Coll. and Univ. Coll., Auckland, N.Z., and Jesus Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1940-46; cadet, Nig., 1940; admin. offr., cl. II, 1954; i/c local govt. wing, inst. of admin., N. regn., 1954.

ABBOTT, M. J.—b. 1906; ed. King's Sch., Canterbury; barrister. Gray's Inn; mil. serv., 1941-45; lieut.; asst. crown solr., H.K., 1936; secon. to Ethiopian Govt. as pres., high ct., 1946; puisne judge, Nig., 1950; judge, high ct. of Lagos and Cams., 1955; fedl. justice, 1957.

ABBOTT, S. H.—b. 1908; ed. Antigua Gram. Sch.; appt. agric. dept., Antigua, 1929; agric. asst., St. Kitts, 1939; farm manager, 1944; agric. offr., Virgin Is., 1946; Nevis, 1949; agric. supt., Antigua, 1955; St. Kitts, 1957.
ABELL, Sir Anthony, K.C.M.G. (1952), C.M.G.—b. 1906; ed. Repton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, Nig., 1929; admin. offr., cl. II, 1946; cl. I, 1948; gov. and c.-in-c., Sarawak, 1950.

ABERNETHY, J. S.—b. 1907; ed. Sedbergh Sch., Aberdeen Gram. Sch., Aberdeen and Edin. Univs.; interned, 1942-45; chartered co., N. Borneo; legal advr., comsnr. of lands, prot. of labr. (incl. inspr., schs.), 1936; food contrlr. (in addn.), 1941; sessions judge, B.M.A. (B. Borneo), 1946; comsnr., lands, 1947; sessions judge, 1948; res. mag., Tang., 1949; judge of high ct., 1951.

ABRAHAM, A. A.—b. 1912; ed. St. Stanislaus Coll., B. Guiana; cler. asst., B. Guiana, 1929; dist. offr., 1945; estab. offr., 1947; prin. ass't. sec., 1953; ch. estab. offr., mem. publ. serv. comsn., 1955.
ABRAHAM, A. L., M.V.O. (1954), Q.P.M. (1957), C.P.M.—b. 1908; ed. Southend High Sch.; police, Pal., 1930; supt., Barb., 1938; Fiji, 1944; B. Hond., 1950; comsnr. police, Gib., 1953.
ABRAHAM, E. E.—b. 1900; ed. Royal Coll., Maur.; writer, treasy., Maur., 1920; clk., govt. depts., 1928-51; chief exec. offr., educ. dept., 1951; asst. sec., sec't, 1955.
ABRAHAMS, A. R.—b. 1908; ed. J'ca Coll.; apptd. J'ca, 1927; pensions offr., 1944; asst. sec., 1950; prin., 1952; perm. sec., min. of commms. and wks., 1954.

ABRAHAMS, H. A.—b. 1906; ed. Calabar High Sch., J'ca.; asst., constab., J'ca., 1923; 2nd cl. clk., constab., 1933; 1st cl. clk., med. dept., 1939; exam. acct., audit dept., 1944; acctnt., dept. of agric., 1948; senr. acctnt., 1950; admin. sec., med. dept., 1952; man., govt. savings bank, 1955; prin. asst. sec., 1955.
ABRAMS, P. D.—b. 1921; ed. Kitale Sch. and Prince of Wales Sch., Ken., and Merton Coll., Oxford; admin. cadet, Ken., 1946; dist. offr., 1948.
ACTON, H. A. G.—b. 1914; ed. Eton, and Magdalene Coll., Camb.; I.C.S., 1937-47; admin. offr., Nig., 1948; cl. II, Fed. of Nig., 1955; cl. I, 1956.
ADAMES, P.—b. 1913; ed. Cranleigh Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford, Camb. Univ. and I.C.T.A.; agric. offr., S.L., 1940; prin. agric. offr., 1951; asst. dir., agric., W. Nig., 1956.
ADAMS, A. B.—b. 1899; ed. Wolmer's Sch., J'ca, London and Illinois Univs.; senr. master, tech. sch., San Fernando, Trin., 1954; prin., 1955.
ADAMS, H. L., T.D.—b. 1910; ed. Haileybury; mil. serv., 1939-45, lt.-col.; cadet, Ken., 1946; sec. comrce. and industry, 1949; econ. sec., E.A.H.C., 1954.
ADAMS, J. E. M.—b. 1923; ed. Modern High Sch., B. Guiana; clk., P.O. B. Guiana, 1943; asst. inspr., labr., 1949; inspr., labr., 1953; labr. comsnr., B. Hond., 1957.

ADAMS, P. F., O.B.E., Medal for Merit (U.S.A.)—b. 1914; ed. Mitcham Secondary Sch. and Lond. Univ.; I.C.S., 1937-47; col. admin. serv., sec. to devel. comsnr., Tang., 1948; prin. asst. sec. (devel.), 1950; M.C.S., cl. IC, ag. contrlr., econ. division, 1953; mem., jt. working party, rubber industry, 1953-54; leader, Mal. reps., inter. rubber study group mtg., Colombo, 1954.
ADAMS, R. K.—b. 1911; asst. registr., co-op. socs., S.L., 1939; Nig., 1946; S.L., 1950.
ADAMSON, A. R.—b. 1916; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin., and Portsmouth Municipal Coll.; exec. engnr., Trin., 1947; asst. D.P.W., B.G., 1958.

ADAMSON, E.—b. 1921; ed. Bootle Gram. Sch. and L'pool Univ.; mil. serv., 1943-46; junr. scien. offr., min. of aircraft prod., 1942; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Mal., 1946; senr. exec. engnr., P.W.D., S'pore, 1955; chief engnr., 1957.

ADAMSON, F. G.—b. 1915; ed. Nottingham High Sch.; solvr.; mil. serv., 1939-45, capt.; asst. comsnt., lands, G.C., 1946; registr., titles, Kent., 1950; asst. registr-gen., Tang., 1951; registr-gen., 1957.
ADAMSON, G. A. G., M.B.E.—b. 1906; mil. serv., 1940-41; game ranger, Ken., 1939; senr., 1955.

ADDI, M.—b. 1917; ed. Bishop Gobat Sch., Jerusalem; posts and tels. depts., Pal., 1936-48; Pal. accts. clearance office, Cyp., 1948-49; C.O., 1949-50; asst. postmaster, Nyasa., 1950; asst. acctnt., 1952; acctnt., 1955; secon., Fed. of Rhod. and Nyasa., 1954-56; acctnt., posts and tels., dept., N. Borneo, 1957.

ADDISON, G. H.—b. 1911; ed. Kelsick Sch., Ambleside, R. Botanic Gdns., Kew, and Berlin Botanic Gdns.; mil. serv., 1941-46; curator, botanic gdns., S'pore, 1938; jt. author Malayan Orchid Hybrids (1956), etc.

ADDISON, W. R. L., M.B.E. (1956), M.C.—b. 1919; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1939-46, maj.; cadet, Uganda, 1948; A.D.O., 1950; asst. sec., E.A.H.C., 1952; clk., cent. leg. assembly, 1951-55; chief sec., Ken., treasy., 1957.

ADEBO, S. O.—b. 1913; ed. St. Peter's Sch., Ake, Abeokuta Gram. Sch., and King's Coll., Lagos; cler. serv., Nig., 1933-42; admin. offr., 1942; asst. fin. sec., 1954; admin. offr., cl. I, W. Nig., 1955; perm. sec., min. of finance, 1957; jt. author Review of Nigerianisation Policy and Practice, 1953.
ADEDIPE, B. A.—b. 1912; ed. St. Gregory's Coll., Lagos; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cler. serv., Nig., 1933; inspr., police, 1945; asst. supt., 1949; crown counsel, 1950; senr., 1957.

ADEMOLA, Sir A. A., Kt. (1957).—b. 1906; ed. King's Coll., Lagos, and Camb. Univ.; barrister-at-law; mag., Nig., 1939; puisne judge, 1949; ch. justice, W. regn., 1955; mem. of comsns. for revision of cts. legn. (1948) and to enq. into Enugu disturbances (1949), Nig.

ADIE, J. J., Brill. Star of Zanz. (4th cl.) (1947)—b. 1913; ed. Kingsland Grange Prep. Sch., Shrewsbury Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-42 (K.A.R. and O.E.T.A.); admin. offr., Zanz., 1937; asst. dist. comsnr., Pemba, 1938; Zanz., 1939; p.s. to Br. Res., 1939-40; asst. sec., 1942; senr. asst. sec. and p.s. to H.H. the Sultan, 1946; secon. C.O., 1949; sec. educ., labr. and lands, Ken., 1952; chief sec., Barb., 1957; perm. sec., Ken., 1958; author of various articles on Zanzibar.

AGG, A. D.—b. 1912; ed. S. A. Collegiate Sch., Cape Town; mil. serv., 1939-40, 2nd lieut.; arch. asst., P.W.D., N. Rhod., 1938; asst. archt., 1945; archt., 1949; asst. D.P.W. (buildings), Nyasa, 1955.
AGOSTINI, C.—b. 1914; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; control offr., Trin., 1941; dep. aerodrome supt., 1946; dir. of civil aviation, and aerodrome supt., 1947.

AGOSTINI, J. E.—b. 1906; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; cler. appts., govt. depts., Trin., 1924-44; senr. clk., acctnt-gen.'s dept., 1945; police (immig.), 1947; prin. offr., 1949; asst. ch. immig. offr., 1952; dep. ch. immig. offr., 1954.
AGROTIS, M. S.—b. 1913; ed. American Univ., Beirut; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; registr., T.U.S., Cyp., 1942; off. receiver and registr., 1948.
AHMED, M. H. C.—b. 1913; prob. labr. inspr., Maur., 1938; labr. inspr., 1940; labr. field offr., 1946; asst. labr. comsnr., 1950.

AHWAI, C.—b. 1920; ed. St. Joseph's Coll., H. Kong, and London Univ.; 2nd cl. clk., Trin., 1940; govt. shorthand writer, 1944; senr. clk., sec't., 1949; prin. offr. (sec. to public serv. comsn.), 1950; asst. sec., 1954.

AINLEY, Sir John, Kt. (1957), M.C.—b. 1906; ed. St. Bees Sch. and Oxford Univ.; barrister-at-law; mil. serv., 1939-45, maj.; dist. mag., G.C., 1933; cr. coun., 1936; puisne judge, Uga., 1946; ch. just., E. Nig., 1956.
AITKEN, I. H.—b. 1919; ed. Greenock High Sch. and St. Andrews Univ.; tax assessor, Ken., 1944; senr. immig. offr., 1948; dep. prin. immig. offr., 1952.

AITKEN, Maj. R.—b. 1910; ed. Gregg Comm. Coll., Ipswich; mil. serv., 1939-46, major; H.M. Prison Service, 1936; sec'n. supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1946; resig.; N.Z. Prisons Serv., 1947; re-appointed supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1949; dep. dir. of prisons, J'ca, 1951; supt., prisons, B. Guiana, 1955.
AITKEN, W. G.—b. 1923; ed. Glasgow High Sch. and Glasgow Univ.; mil. serv., 1942-45, lieut.; geologist, Tang., 1947; author of various pp. on post-Karroo geology of Tang.
AKENHEAD, E., T.D.—b. 1913; ed. Rugby Sch.; solicitor; mil. serv., 1939-45; asst. land offr., Tang., 1946; senr. asst. land offr., 1957.
AKERROYD, A.—b. 1911; ed. St. Luke's Sch. and Cockburn High Sch., Leeds; architect, N. Rhodesia, 1947; senr. architect, 1955.

AKERS, R. L.—b. 1912; ed. Solihull Sch. and Birmingham Univ.; mil. serv., 1941-42, 1944-46, capt.; asst. engrn., drainage and irrig., F.M.S., 1936; senr. irrig. engrn., S. Leone, 1943; engrn., drainage and irrig., Mal., 1946; state engrn., gr. B., 1949; chief construc. engrn., drainage and irrig., Fed. of Mal., 1951; dep. dir., drainage and irrig., 1955.
ALAKIJA, O. B.—b. 1910; ed. C.M.S. Gram. Sch. and King's Coll., Lagos, Seaford Coll. and Guy's Hosp., London; house physician and surgeon, Nig., 1939; M.O., 1940; S.M.O. (admin.) 1952.
ALBURY, K. H. E.—b. 1920; ed. Govt. High Sch., Nassau; clk., public treasy., Bah., 1940; asst. audr., 1950; audr., 1956.
ALCINDOR, F.—b. 1909; ed. Ideal High Sch., Trin.; barrister-at-law; apptd. Trin., 1933; clk., magistracy, 1940-49; senr. clk., 1949; assessor, inland rev., 1954; magistrate, 1954.

ALCOCK, R. N., C.P.M. (1952),—b. 1912; ed. Highbury Sch., King Edward VII Nautical Sch., London, and Hendon Police Coll.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; police cadet, Nig., 1938; asst. supt., 1941; secon. S. Leone, 1943-44; senr. asst. supt., 1947; senr. supt., police, 1950; crown coun., N. Nig., 1955; publ. Police Instruction Book (3 edns.).
ALDERDICE, A. A.—b. 1912; ed. Sydney Gram. Sch., Univ. of Sydney, and London Hosp.; mil. serv., 1940-45, lt.-col.; M.O., gr. I, Trin., 1947; med. supt., Uga., 1950; senr. specialist, 1954.

ALDERTON, E. C.—b. 1908; ed. Sherborne Sch. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1939-45, lt.-col.; cadet, Nig., 1931; admin. offr., cl. II, 1950; cl. I, 1954; perm. sec., min. of internal affrs., E. regn., 1954; sec. to premier, 1957.

ALDOUS, L. W.—b. 1913; ed. Stationers' Co. Sch., and L.S.I., London Univ.; mil. serv., 1939-45, capt.; F.O. (German section) control comsn. for Germany, 1946-49; devl. asst., Uga., 1952; sec., 1954; senr. asst. sec., 1955.

ALDRIDGE, A. S., O.B.E. (1955), M.C., Cross of Valour (Poland).—b. 1918; ed. Durham Sch.; mil. serv., 1939-46, maj.; admin. offr., Cyp., 1946; cl. II, 1951; cl. I, 1953; senr. admin. offr., 1954; secon., C.O., 1955; under-sec. (internal security), Cyprus, 1955; secon., C.O., 1956.
ALEONG, R. V. S.—b. 1911; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin., Faraday Hse. Elec. Engrng. Coll., London; chief electrical inspector, Trin., 1950.

ALEXANDER, A. L., Q.P.M. (1955), C.P.M. (1951),—b. 1907; ed. Wellingborough Sch.; Pal. police, 1931; sub-inspr., Trin., 1935; G.C., 1946; senr. asst. supt., 1948; asst. comsnr., 1949; dep. comsnr. (Ghana civil service), 1955.

ALEXANDER, D. A. R.—b. 1920; ed. St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, and Univ. Coll., London; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; dep. clk. of courts, J'ca, 1944; mag., Turks and Caicos Is., 1947; clk. of courts, J'ca, 1949; asst. legal dftsmn., 1951; legal dftsmn., 1955; W. Nig., 1957.

ALEXANDER, J. B.—b. 1912; ed. Tiffin Boys' Sch., Kingston-on-Thames, Imp. Coll. of Science and Tech., London Univ. and California Inst. of Tech., U.S.A.; interned, 1942-45; geologist, Nyasa., 1935; F.M.S., 1939; prin. geol., 1950.

ALEXANDER, J. L. N.—b. 1907; ed. Tavistock Gram. Sch. and Birmingham Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-45, capt. (desps. twice); water and drainage engrn., P.W.D., Uga., 1946; redesign. chief water and drainage engrn., 1953.

ALEXANDER, W. G., M.B.E.—b. 1918; ed. Sedbergh Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-45, major; admin. cadet, G. & E. Is. Col., 1946; co-op. soc. offr., 1947; registr., co-op. soc., Maur., 1951; comsnr., co-op. devel., Cyp., 1955; U.K. delegate, F.A.O./I.L.O. tech. mtg. on co-ops. in Asia and F.E., 1954; survey and Report on possibilities co-op. dev. and necessary co-op. legislation in G. & E. Is., 1947.
ALEXANDER, W. H.—b. 1907; ed. Wolmer's Sch., J'ca; barrister-at-law; apptd. J'ca, 1927; dep. clk., 1939; clk. of courts, 1947; crown coun., 1950; senr., 1955; resdt. mag., 1955.
ALEXIS, F. C.—b. 1913; ed. Grenada Boys' Secondary Sch. and Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Edin.; dist. med. offr., St. V., 1935; supt. physician, col. hosp., Grenada, 1932; S.M.O., 1955.

ALFORD, R. E., C.M.G.—b. 1904; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; nav. serv., 1940-45; cadet, Nig., 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1931; dist. offr. (clk., exec. and leg. couns.), 1938; senr. dist. offr., 1946; fin. sec., Zanz., 1947; ch. sec., 1952; gov. and c.-in-c., St. Helena, 1958.
ALI, Ayub, O.B.E. (1956), M.B.E.—b. 1907; ed. Govt. Indian Sch., Nairobi; clk., sect., Ken., 1920; asst. estab. offr., 1944; estab. offr., 1948; asst. sec. (estab.), 1955.

ALLAN, C. H.—b. 1921; ed. Hamilton High Sch., Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z., and Camb. Univ.; mil. serv., 1942-44; cadet, B.S.I.P., 1945; admin. offr., cl. B., 1949; cl. A., 1955; secon., spec. lands comsnr., W. Pac. H.C., 1956.
ALLAN, D. A. G., M.C.—b. 1918; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. Univ.; mil. serv., 1939-47; exec. engnr., Ken., 1948; senr. exec. engnr., Nyasa., 1956.

ALLAN, J. A.—b. 1910; ed. Glasgow Univ. and W. of Scotland Agric. Coll.; lectr., Barb., 1936; tech. offr., Nat. Inst. of Agric. Botany, Camb., 1944; supt., agric., Cyprus, 1945; pasture research offr., Ken., 1947; agric. offr., N. Rhod., 1949.

ALLAN, W., C.B.E. (1954), O.B.E.—b. 1904; ed. North Kelvinside Sch., Glasgow Univ., London Univ. (Wye) and I.C.T.A.; asst. research offr., agric. dept., N. Rhod., 1928; asst. dir., agric. and entom., 1938; dir., agric., Maur., 1948; Cyp., 1954.

ALLEN, C. P. S., M.V.O.—b. 1917; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Sch., N.Z., and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1940-43, K.A.R.; admin. cadet, Uga., 1940; asst. dist. offr., 1942; asst. chief sec., 1953; perm. sec. for security and external relations, 1955; secon., Br. embassy, Washington, 1956-57.

ALLEN, D. J.—b. 1911; ed. Clive Hse. Sch., St. Edward's Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford Univ.; customs prob., F.M.S., 1933; asst. supt., customs and excise, 1936; customs offr., 1939; senr., 1939; contr., supplies, Aden, 1941-44; asst. compt., customs, Fed. of Mal., 1951.

ALLEN, D. M., M.B.E. (1949).—b. 1916; ed. Bloxham and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; cadet, admin., G.C., 1938; asst. dist. comsnr., 1939; dist. comsnr., 1945; admin. offr., cl. II, 1950; cl. I, 1952; perm. sec. (Ghana civil service), 1956.

ALLEN, E. C., O.B.E.—b. 1902; ed. Bradford Commercial Sch. and Sch. of Accountancy; clk., sect., and reporter, ag. co., Nyasa., 1925; asst. treas., Tang., 1930; senr., 1940; secon. as treas., ch. of customs, British Som., 1940; fin. sec. and offr. of inc. tax, Basuto., 1945.
ALLEN, H. P.—b. 1903; ed. Cornwall Coll., Ica, and L'pool Univ.; barrister-at-law; clk. of courts, Ica, 1936; resdt. mag., 1939.

ALLEN, J. G. C., C.M.G. (1956).—b. 1904; ed. Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, Ecole Superieure de Commerce, Lausanne, and Munich Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-43, maj.; cadet, Nig., 1926; senr. dist. offr., 1945; cl. I, 1947; admin. offr., staff gr., 1953; E. regn., 1955; Br. comsnr., Anglo-French Cameroons Boundary Comsn., 1937-39; Nig. govt. rep. to Free French, Duala, 1940; i/c. mil. intell. and censorship, Nig., 1940-41; liaison offr. to Gen. Leclerc, 1942; mem., res. min's staff, Accra, 1943; labour offr., 1943-44; off. "goodwill" visit, Algeria and Morocco, 1949.
ALLEN, J. W. H.—b. 1913; ed. Regent St. Poly. and Downing Coll., Camb.; asst. audr., Nig., 1936; C.A.D., 1938; Fiji, 1943; senr. audr., Mal., 1948; dep. dir., fed. audit., Nig., 1954; dir., 1958.

ALLEN, J. W. T.—b. 1904; ed. Westminster and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educ., T.T., 1929; asst. dist. offr., 1937; dist. offr., 1942; political offr., Aden, 1947; dep. Br. agt., W. Aden Prot., 1948; D.O., Tang., 1953; senr., 1954; admin. offr., cl. IIA, 1957.

ALLEN, R. L.—b. 1914; ed. Coombe Down C. of E. Sch.; Br. P.O. 1942-47; Sudan govt. posts and tels. dept., 1945; E.A. posts and tels. dept., techn., gr. I, 1947; insptg. techn., 1949; supvg. techn., 1954.

ALLEYNE, K. H. C., Q.C.—b. 1918; ed. Dominica Gram. Sch., Univ. Coll., London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Devonshire 2nd course); barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; registr., Dominica, 1943; mag., St. L., 1944; cr. atty., 1949; St. V., 1952; atty.-gen., Wind. Is., 1956; author of Memoir of constitutional development of St. Lucia, 1950.
ALLIN, P. W. M.—b. 1906; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal, and Rhodes Univ., Capetown; land survr., N. Rhod., 1930; senr. survr., 1945; dep. dir., surv. and land, 1951; survr.-gen., 1957.

ALLISON, J. A.—b. 1915; ed. St. John's Coll., Jo'burg, and Witwatersrand Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-46, maj.; admin. offr., cl. IV, Nig., 1938; D.O., 1946; Bech. Prot., 1950; high comsnr's. office, 1950-51.
ALLISON, P. A.—b. 1907; ed. Caterham Sch. and St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; asst. consvr., forests, Nig., 1931; senr. asst., 1949; consvr., 1954.
ALLPRESS, P. L.—b. 1921; ed. Beaumont Coll. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1941-42, 2nd lieut.; cadet, Nig., 1942; admin. offr., cl. II, E. Nig., 1955; cl. I, 1958.

ALLSEBROOK, G. P.—b. 1918; ed. Stowe Sch., Kent Sch., Conn., U.S.A. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law; mil. serv., 1939-43, capt.; cadet, Tang., 1940; dist. offr., 1942; secon., adminr., Virgin Is., 1956.

ALLWRIGHT, W. C.—b. 1915; ed. Rutlish Sch., Merton, and King's Coll. Hosp., Univ. of London; mil. serv., 1940-46, sqdn. ldr.; dental surg., H.K., 1947; senr., 1949; redesig. dental specialist, 1951; H.K. rep. at W.H.O. dental health seminar, N.Z., 1954; author Suppurative Osteomyelitis of Mandible (Dental Prac., 1953).

ALMAN, J. H.—b. 1919; ed. Chester Coll., Birkenhead, and L'pool Univ.; mil. serv., 1939-46, major (desps.); educ. offr., Mal., 1946; prin., Kent teachers train. coll., N. Borneo, 1953; author, Malaya school text book series.
ALP, W. C.—b. 1907; ed. Christ's Hosp. and London Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-45; archt., P.W.D., Ken., 1948.
ALTORFER, A.—b. 1915; ed. Calday Grange Gram. Sch.; mil. serv. 1940-46; prin. local govt. inspr., Ken., 1950.
ALVARES, R. E.—b. 1909; ed. St. Joseph's Coll., H.K. and H.K. Univ.; asst. pathologist, H.K., 1939; pathologist, 1951.
AMAH, D. J.—b. 1916; ed. King's Coll., Lagos, and St. Andrews Univ.; M.O., Nig., 1944; E. Nig.
AMAR, G. S.—b. 1912; ed. Punjab Univ.; asst. master, Ken., 1942; educ. offr. (Asian), 1947; senr., 1949.

AMATO-GAUCI, F. E., M.B.E. (mil.) (1945)—b. 1917; ed. St. John Military and Royal Univ. of Malta; mil. serv., 1939-45, maj.; civ. serv. (admin.), Malta, 1936; prin. offr., 1948; dir. of labr., 1949; dir., labr. and soc. welf., 1955; dir., emig., labr. and soc. welf., 1956; (also ex off. registr., trade unions, and chmn., labr. bd., youth advsy. comtee., port workers' joint coun., port workers' bd.).

AMBIAVAGAR, V.—b. 1907; ed. Methodist Boys Sch., K. Lumpur and Raffles Instn., and Raffles Coll., S'pore; pupil teacher, S'pore, 1925; cl. I. teacher, 1928; graduate teacher, 1931; spec. teacher (English), gr.I, 1950; prin., Beatty secondary sch., 1954; dep. sec., min. of educ., 1955.

AMBROSE, J. W. D.—b. 1909; ed. Free Sch., Penang and Oxford Univ.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; asst. offl. assignee, S'pore, 1936; police mag. and asst. dist. judge, Malacca, 1940; registr., superior ct., 1945; dep. publ. prosecutor, N. Sembilan, 1946; senr. asst. registr., supreme ets. of Ipoh, Penang, K. Lumpur, 1947-52; pres., sessions ct., Penang, 1953; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., S'pore, 1955; off. assignee, public trustee, comsnr. of estate duties, etc., 1957.

AMOROSO-CENTENO, A. E. B., M.B.E.—b. 1910; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Port-of-Spain; called to bar, Gray's Inn; clk., postal, treas. and acctnt.-gen.'s depts., Trin., 1928-42; assessor, inl. rev. dept., 1943; sec., 1950; dep. comsnr., 1952; comsnr., 1957.

AMOROSO-CENTENO, M.—b. 1908; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; clk., customs and excise, Trin., 1928; senr. offr., 1940; prin. offr., 1943; supvr., cl. II, 1946; cl. I, 1948; asst. compt., customs and excise, 1954; dep. compt., 1956.
